ai_strategy_lead.name: Wei Ding
ai_strategy_lead.title: Distinguished Professor of Computer Science; Executive Director, Paul English Applied AI Institute

documents.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Guidance for Learning and Teaching
Faculty Council Syllabus Guidelines on AI Use

ai_strategy.summary: UMass Boston has not published a single unified institutional AI strategy document, but its public AI posture is organized around the Paul English Applied AI Institute (launched 2023 with a $5M gift plus a $2M UMass Foundation match) and learning-and-teaching guidance issued through Instructional Design Services. The Institute is positioned as the nation's first university-based institute dedicated to 'optimizing and democratizing' applied AI across every profession, with explicit emphasis on social, ethical, and diversity dimensions. Enterprise tooling is coordinated through the UMass system's campusGenAI platform and the UMass Boston license for Microsoft Copilot Chat.

key_provisions: Faculty determine their own course-level AI policy
Syllabus must include one of two Faculty Council-recommended statements (AI prohibited, or AI allowed with attribution)
Using AI without proper attribution qualifies as academic dishonesty
AI-generated content must be cited with full LLM output attached as appendix when permitted
Users must not enter unpublished or proprietary data into AI tools
AI-generated content shared externally must be clearly labeled as such
Microsoft Copilot Chat is the licensed enterprise tool protected under UMass Microsoft 365 compliance
ai_policy.summary: UMass Boston's AI policy, recommended by the Faculty Council at its October 2023 meeting, leaves course-level decisions to individual faculty while requiring a syllabus-level statement choosing between a prohibitive or attribution-with-citation stance. University-wide guidance layers in data privacy cautions, ethics expectations around proper licensing and clear labeling, and a designation of Microsoft Copilot Chat as the sanctioned enterprise tool.

bodies.name: Paul English Applied AI Institute Lead Planning Team
bodies.scope: Cross-college planning team (6 members) guiding institute strategy, curriculum, and community engagement across UMass Boston's six colleges.

bodies.name: Paul English Applied AI Institute Student Advisory Council
bodies.scope: 15-member council of undergraduates, graduate students, and doctoral candidates from five of six colleges providing student voice on AI programming.

bodies.name: Faculty Council (AI syllabus and academic integrity)
bodies.scope: Issued the October 2023 recommendation requiring an AI statement on every syllabus and framing permissible versus prohibited AI use for coursework.

bodies.name: Applied Ethics Center / Institute for Ethics and Emerging Technologies
bodies.scope: Runs the Ethics of Emerging Technologies research collaboration on AI, funds post-doctoral fellowships, and hosts public lectures, conferences, and symposia on AI ethics and politics.

governance_structure.summary: Governance is distributed rather than centralized in a chief AI officer. The Paul English Applied AI Institute provides programmatic leadership through a Lead Planning Team and a Student Advisory Council; the Faculty Council owns course-level policy; and the Applied Ethics Center hosts ethics research and public engagement. At the system level, UMass participates in the Massachusetts AI Strategic Task Force through UMass Chief Policy and Operations Officer Mike Milligan.

initiatives.title: Paul English Applied Artificial Intelligence Institute
initiatives.description: Launched with a $5 million gift from entrepreneur and UMass Boston alumnus Paul English (BS 1987, MS 1989) plus a $2 million UMass Foundation match, the Institute describes itself as the first university-based institute in the nation dedicated to optimizing and democratizing applied AI across every profession. It embeds AI learning, faculty development, and student research in every college, with emphasis on social, ethical, and diversity implications.
initiatives.significance: A naming-gift-funded, cross-disciplinary applied AI institute at an R1 minority-serving urban public university is a distinctive model for sector-wide democratization of AI skills, contrasting with the narrower STEM-center model used at many private research universities.

investment.funding_source: Paul English ($5M) and UMass Foundation match ($2M)
investment.description: $5M benefactor gift from Paul English plus $2M UMass Foundation match launching the institute in AY 2023-24.

collaborations.description: Benefactor-driven founding; industry speaker series with partners such as EvenUp's Haixun Wang.

initiatives.policy_implications: Institute carries explicit mandate to surface ethical, social, and diversity implications of AI deployment rather than purely technical capability building.

initiatives.title: Faculty Council AI Syllabus Statement Requirement
initiatives.description: At its October 2023 meeting, the Faculty Council recommended that every syllabus include one of two statements: (1) AI is prohibited and all work must be student-generated, or (2) AI is allowed on specified assignments with full citation including in-text, references, and an appendix containing the full LLM output. Using AI without attribution is academic dishonesty.
initiatives.significance: Represents one of the earliest R1-level adoptions of mandatory syllabus-level AI disclosure coupled with a pluralistic 'faculty discretion with attribution' model that balances instructor autonomy with institutional transparency.

investment.description: Policy development within existing Faculty Council and Provost office resources.

initiatives.policy_implications: Normalizes course-level AI disclosure while leaving substantive judgment to individual faculty. Binds students to explicit appendix-level disclosure of LLM outputs.

initiatives.title: Networked AI Lab, College of Science and Mathematics
initiatives.description: Established June 2025 with a $439,695 award from the U.S. Department of Defense, led by Associate Professor Bo Sheng. The lab develops collaborative autonomous systems that combine self-driving cars with LiDAR and drones with 360-degree cameras for transportation safety, emergency response, environmental monitoring, and defense. A core technique is federated learning.
initiatives.significance: A DoD-funded networked-AI lab at a newly classified R1 expands public-sector applied AI research capacity beyond the large R1 incumbents and builds federated learning expertise in an urban minority-serving context.

investment.description: DoD research award funding the Networked AI Lab setup and research activity.

collaborations.description: DoD-funded research on networked autonomous systems and federated learning.

initiatives.title: Boston Public Schools AI Literacy Partnership
initiatives.description: Announced March 2026, UMass Boston and Boston Public Schools launched a public-private partnership backed by a $1 million gift from Paul English (channeled through the Paul English Applied AI Institute) to bring AI literacy to 20 BPS high schools and eventually the whole district. Scope includes teacher professional development, industry-informed curriculum, and student internships, aiming to make Boston the first major U.S. city where every public high-school graduate has AI proficiency.
initiatives.significance: Positions a public R1 as the anchor higher-ed partner for K-12 AI literacy at citywide scale, a model for university-city-industry collaboration on pre-college AI workforce readiness.

investment.description: $1M philanthropic gift from Paul English underwriting the BPS AI literacy partnership.

collaborations.description: University-city-donor partnership to deliver AI curriculum, teacher training, and internships across BPS.

initiatives.policy_implications: Framed explicitly around educational equity and democratization of AI access across a majority minority urban school district.

initiatives.title: Microsoft Copilot Chat Enterprise Deployment
initiatives.description: UMass Boston has licensed the enterprise version of Microsoft Copilot Chat for faculty, staff, and students, accessible via m365.cloud.microsoft/chat with UMass Boston SSO. Data is protected under UMass Microsoft 365 compliance: prompts and responses are not used to train public models and are tied to the UMass Boston tenant. Currently scoped to public-web grounding; Copilot does not yet search OneDrive, SharePoint, or Outlook data.
initiatives.significance: Represents the university's campus-wide, identity-bound enterprise GenAI platform baseline, a common R1 approach but paired here with explicit constraints on tenant-data access.

investment.funding_source: UMass Boston IT operating budget / Microsoft 365 licensing
investment.description: Enterprise license bundled with Microsoft 365 tenant; specific cost not disclosed.

tools_and_vendors.name: Microsoft Copilot Chat
tools_and_vendors.role: Enterprise generative AI chat for faculty, staff, and students

initiatives.policy_implications: Scoped to public-web grounding only, preserving tenant data isolation as a conservative default while broader enterprise grounding is evaluated.

initiatives.title: UMass System campusGenAI Platform Participation
initiatives.description: UMass Boston participates in campusGenAI, the UMass system's shared GenAI platform (campusgenai.org domain) that provides students, faculty, and staff a secure environment to access leading AI models including Anthropic, Meta, and OpenAI via a unified chat interface. The system is managed by UMass Amherst's UITS group with an umpo_ai_hub@umassp.edu coordinating address.
initiatives.significance: Demonstrates system-level AI procurement and governance coordination across the UMass campuses, amortizing model access and security review across five R1/R2 institutions.

investment.funding_source: UMass Office of the President / UITS
investment.description: System-wide shared service; per-campus cost not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Shared UMass system GenAI platform procurement and governance.
Model access via the multi-vendor campusGenAI chat interface.

initiatives.policy_implications: Users advised against entering personal or confidential information; prompts are saved and may be reviewed.

initiatives.title: AI Bootcamp Programs (Prompt Engineering, AI Agents & Workflow Automation)
initiatives.description: UMass Boston's continuing education unit, in partnership with Upright Education, offers non-credit AI bootcamps that include AI Prompt Engineering and AI Agents & Workflow Automation. The programs target working professionals and award university-backed certificates.
initiatives.significance: Extends the university's workforce-readiness mission into adjacent professional audiences with short-cycle, industry-aligned AI credentials.

collaborations.description: Industry partner provides curriculum and delivery for the AI bootcamps.

initiatives.title: High School AI Summer Research Camp
initiatives.description: The Paul English Applied AI Institute launched its inaugural high school AI Summer Research Camp in August 2025 (running August 4-22, 2025), with a second iteration scheduled for summer 2026. The program is delivered through the College of Science and Mathematics via UMass Boston's professional and continuing education platform.
initiatives.significance: Builds an early pipeline into an urban R1's applied AI ecosystem, targeting a diverse high school population as a feeder to both the BPS AI literacy initiative and UMass Boston enrollment.

investment.description: Funded through institute endowment and program fees.

collaborations.description: Two-week summer camp recruiting high school students into applied AI research.

initiatives.title: Ethics of Emerging Technologies AI Collaboration
initiatives.description: A long-term collaboration between UMass Boston's Applied Ethics Center and the Institute for Ethics and Emerging Technologies focused on the ethics and politics of AI. Includes three post-doctoral fellowships, public lectures, and a series of conferences and symposia on algorithmic bias, fairness, and AI governance. The Applied Ethics Center also runs Tech Talk with the City of Boston, bringing faculty and youth leaders together on AI, social media, and work.
initiatives.significance: Anchors an ethics-first research agenda at an R1 minority-serving urban university, pairing academic inquiry with public youth-facing engagement on algorithmic fairness.

investment.funding_source: Applied Ethics Center / Institute for Ethics and Emerging Technologies
investment.description: Funds three post-doctoral fellowships plus public programming.

partners: Institute for Ethics and Emerging Technologies
collaborations.description: Ethics center partnership and city-academic Tech Talk youth programming.

initiatives.policy_implications: Research outputs are intended to inform algorithmic fairness and AI governance debate at state and national levels.

initiatives.title: NIH-Funded AI for Human Physical Activity Measurement
initiatives.description: Wei Ding (UMass Boston) and Scott Crouter (University of Tennessee Knoxville) hold an NIH R01 collaborative research grant using AI and machine learning on hip and wrist accelerometer and gyroscope data to classify physical activities (e.g., running vs. jumping). The project aims to support personalized exercise prescriptions and investigate sedentary behavior, physical activity, and health outcomes.
initiatives.significance: Example of R1-level interdisciplinary health-AI research from UMass Boston, combining wearable sensor data and ML for a clinically actionable deliverable.

investment.description: Multi-year NIH R01 research project grant; exact amount not disclosed.

collaborations.description: Joint R01 between UMass Boston (Ding) and UT Knoxville (Crouter).

initiatives.title: Massachusetts AI Strategic Task Force Participation
initiatives.description: The UMass System serves as an academic co-chair (through Mike Milligan) of Governor Healey's Executive Order 629 Artificial Intelligence Strategic Task Force, alongside the City of Boston. Members include representatives from UMass, MIT, BU, Harvard, Northeastern, and Yale. The task force recommended establishing a Massachusetts AI Hub (December 2024), for which Governor Healey is seeking $100 million in economic development legislation. UMass is a member of the MGHPCC consortium that will anchor applied AI work under the Hub.
initiatives.significance: Places UMass Boston inside state-level AI economic development governance through its system affiliation, aligning campus AI activity with a $100M public applied-AI funding pipeline.

investment.funding_source: Commonwealth of Massachusetts (proposed economic development legislation)
investment.description: $100M Applied AI Hub funding sought by Governor Healey to support the Hub's capital grant program.

partners: Massachusetts Green High Performance Computing Center (MGHPCC)
collaborations.description: State task force participation via UMass System; MGHPCC is the shared HPC consortium.
MGHPCC member universities collaborating on sectoral AI applications.

initiatives.policy_implications: UMass Boston's AI activity is tied to state policy direction: any eventual AI Hub grant criteria, state acceptable-use guidance, or sectoral priorities will shape campus strategy.

initiatives.title: Unity HPC and AI Platform Consortium Membership
initiatives.description: UMass Boston is a partner institution in Unity, a shared HPC and AI computing platform hosted at MGHPCC. Other partners include UMass Amherst, UMass Lowell, UMass Dartmouth, University of Rhode Island, and Mount Holyoke College. Unity provides GPU and AI-capable computing for research workloads.
initiatives.significance: Provides UMass Boston researchers access to competitive shared GPU/HPC infrastructure without the full cost of campus ownership, illustrating the consortium model for AI compute at a newly-classified R1.

collaborations.description: Multi-institution shared research computing and AI platform.

computing_infrastructure.supercomputer_name: Unity HPC and AI Platform (shared via MGHPCC)
computing_infrastructure.summary: UMass Boston's primary AI compute is via Unity, a shared HPC/AI platform hosted at MGHPCC with UMass Amherst, UMass Lowell, UMass Dartmouth, URI, and Mount Holyoke. Local AI Core Facility provides additional ML infrastructure. No disclosed campus-owned flagship AI supercomputer.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Paul English Institute AI democratization mandate
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Institute's founding mission explicitly frames AI learning as democratized across every profession and student, rooted in UMass Boston's identity as an urban, minority-serving R1.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Boston Public Schools AI Literacy Initiative
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: $1M Paul English gift channeled through PEAAII underwrites AI curriculum, teacher training, and student internships in 20 BPS high schools, intended to become district-wide.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: CS Department DEI and accessibility commitments
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Computer Science department commits to inclusive design principles, digital accessibility, and DEI in curriculum and pedagogy, including AI coursework.

regulatory_context.summary: Massachusetts established an AI Strategic Task Force via Executive Order 629 (February 2024), co-chaired by EOED and EOTSS with UMass and the City of Boston as academic and local-government co-chairs. The task force recommended a Massachusetts AI Hub (December 2024), and Governor Healey is seeking $100M for Applied AI Hub capital grants. The UMass system provides shared AI governance and tooling through UITS (campusGenAI, MGHPCC/Unity). UMass Boston campus policy is articulated primarily through the Faculty Council October 2023 syllabus statement and Instructional Design Services guidance rather than a standalone state-mandated compliance framework.

narrative_summary:
University of Massachusetts-Boston is a newly minted R1 urban public research university (designated in the 2025 Carnegie classification, with ~15,600 students and FY24 research expenditures of ~$71M) whose AI posture is anchored by the Paul English Applied Artificial Intelligence Institute. Launched in May 2023 with a $5M gift from alumnus Paul English and a $2M UMass Foundation match, PEAAII is positioned as the first university-based institute in the U.S. dedicated to democratizing applied AI across every profession, led by Distinguished Professor Wei Ding. The Institute has rapidly scaled into a flagship civic partnership with Boston Public Schools, announced in March 2026 and funded by a further $1M from English, to bring AI literacy to 20 BPS high schools and eventually the entire district.

On policy, UMass Boston adopted an early and notable Faculty Council resolution (October 2023) requiring every syllabus to include one of two AI statements, coupled with Instructional Design Services guidance that is permissive-with-attribution, privacy-conscious, and explicit on labeling AI-generated content. Enterprise tooling relies on a licensed Microsoft Copilot Chat deployment (public-web-grounded, tenant-isolated) and participation in the UMass system's campusGenAI multi-model platform brokering Anthropic, Meta, and OpenAI access under a shared compliance framework.

Research coverage spans the new DoD-funded Networked AI Lab (2025, $440K, federated learning and collaborative autonomous systems under Bo Sheng), the Knowledge Discovery Lab's NIH-funded health and environmental AI work, and the Applied Ethics Center's long-term AI ethics collaboration producing post-doctoral fellowships and public programming on algorithmic fairness. Compute leverages the MGHPCC-hosted Unity platform shared across UMass, URI, and Mount Holyoke.

Governance is distributed: PEAAII planning and student advisory councils, Faculty Council for course-level policy, Applied Ethics Center for ethics, and UMass system representation on the Massachusetts AI Strategic Task Force and proposed $100M Applied AI Hub. The notable gap is the absence of a standalone published institutional AI strategy document or a named chief AI officer role; strategy is effectively articulated through the Institute's mandate and distributed governance bodies.

gaps_acknowledged: No publicly named chief AI officer or standalone institutional AI strategy document for UMass Boston specifically; strategy is distributed across PEAAII, Faculty Council, and UMass system governance.
Exact deployment date and user count for Microsoft Copilot Chat at UMass Boston not disclosed publicly.
No disclosed GPU count or dedicated campus-owned AI supercomputer; compute is consortial via Unity/MGHPCC.
The CS department page indicated AI coursework embedded in CS/IT majors, but no dedicated AI major, minor, or certificate at the credit-bearing level was found beyond CS 670 and non-credit bootcamps.
Chancellor Marcelo Suarez-Orozco title uses 'chancellor' as the campus executive; the UMass System president is Marty Meehan. Mapped both accordingly.
No student-facing advising chatbot or predictive-retention AI product was surfaced as publicly deployed at UMass Boston.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: balanced enterprise.
